title: Walking in the Rain
summary: A Seattle narrator describes a quiet walk home in soft city rain and why she does not mind getting wet.
body: I walked home in the rain today. It was not a strong rain. It was a soft, quiet rain. My umbrella stayed in my bag. I did not need it. I wore my long blue coat. I wore my brown boots. The streets were shiny and wet. The shop windows had small lights inside. The lights made warm yellow circles on the wet ground. A small black dog ran past me. He was very happy in the rain. A woman with a red umbrella smiled at me. She said, 'Lovely rain today.' I said, 'Yes, it is.' I walked slowly. I listened to the rain on the leaves. I listened to my boots on the path. I stopped near the bakery. The smell of warm bread came out of the door. I bought one small loaf. Then I walked home. My hair was a little wet. My cheeks were cold. But I felt very calm. Some people do not like rain. I love the soft rain in my city.

annotations.exampleEn: A soft rain fell all morning.

annotations.meaningVi: vẫn nằm trong túi (không lấy ra)
annotations.exampleEn: My phone stayed in my bag all day.
She wore her long blue coat.
The streets were shiny and wet after the rain.

annotations.meaningVi: những vòng ánh sáng vàng ấm
annotations.exampleEn: The lamps made warm yellow circles on the floor.
A boy ran past me on the path.

annotations.meaningVi: Mưa hôm nay dễ chịu nhỉ
annotations.exampleEn: She smiled and said, 'Lovely rain today.'
The smell of warm bread filled the shop.
He bought one small loaf for dinner.
Her cheeks were cold from the wind.
After the walk, I felt very calm.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple to tell a small story
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cả đoạn dùng quá khứ đơn vì câu chuyện đã xảy ra: 'I walked home in the rain.' / 'I wore my long blue coat.' / 'A woman with a red umbrella smiled at me.' Quá khứ bất quy tắc trong bài: wore (wear), ran (run), came (come), bought (buy), felt (feel).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The whole text uses simple past because it already happened: 'I walked home in the rain.' / 'I wore my long blue coat.' / 'A woman with a red umbrella smiled at me.' Irregular past forms in the text: wore, ran, came, bought, felt.

grammarNotes.title: Sense verbs: see, hear, smell
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi nói về cảm nhận, ta hay dùng các động từ giác quan: 'I listened to the rain on the leaves.' / 'The smell of warm bread came out of the door.' Lưu ý 'listen to' luôn đi với 'to'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use sense verbs to describe what we notice: 'I listened to the rain on the leaves.' / 'The smell of warm bread came out of the door.' Note: 'listen to' always uses 'to'.

translationVi: Hôm nay tôi đi bộ về nhà dưới cơn mưa. Không phải mưa to. Đó là một cơn mưa nhẹ và yên ả. Cái ô vẫn nằm trong túi tôi. Tôi không cần đến nó. Tôi mặc cái áo khoác dài màu xanh. Tôi đi đôi bốt nâu. Đường phố sáng bóng và ướt. Trong cửa sổ các cửa hàng có những bóng đèn nhỏ. Ánh đèn tạo những vòng vàng ấm áp trên mặt đường ướt. Một con chó nhỏ màu đen chạy ngang qua tôi. Nó rất vui dưới mưa. Một người phụ nữ cầm ô đỏ cười với tôi. Cô ấy nói: 'Mưa hôm nay dễ chịu nhỉ.' Tôi đáp: 'Vâng đúng vậy.' Tôi đi chậm. Tôi lắng nghe tiếng mưa rơi trên lá. Tôi lắng nghe tiếng bốt mình trên đường. Tôi dừng lại gần tiệm bánh. Mùi bánh mì ấm bay ra từ cửa. Tôi mua một ổ nhỏ. Rồi tôi đi tiếp về nhà. Tóc tôi hơi ướt. Hai má tôi lạnh. Nhưng tôi cảm thấy rất bình yên. Có người không thích mưa. Còn tôi yêu cơn mưa nhẹ của thành phố mình.

questions.prompt: What kind of rain was it?
Why did the umbrella stay in the bag?
What did the shop window lights look like?
questions.explanation: 'warm yellow circles on the wet ground.'
'A small black dog ran past me.'

questions.prompt: What did the woman with a red umbrella say?
What did the narrator listen to?
options: The rain on the leaves and her boots on the path
questions.explanation: 'I listened to the rain on the leaves. I listened to my boots on the path.'

questions.prompt: How did her hair look at the end?
How did she feel after the walk?

options: The narrator loves the soft rain in her city
She wants to move to a dry city
questions.explanation: 'I love the soft rain in my city.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some people see rain as bad weather; others find it calming. What kind of weather makes you feel calm, and why?
